hook: Small model. Local agent. Actually ships code without a GPU.
intro_context: acme/coder-model is a compact coding model on HuggingFace designed for local agentic workflows, clustered with the acme/agent-repo tool harness and a paper on practical agentic pipelines. The pitch: a model small enough to run on edge hardware that still produces useful code edits.
three_key_points: Lean coding model tuned for agent-in-the-loop edits, not chat completions.
Runs on commodity hardware — no A100 required, tested on a Pi 4.
Clusters with acme/agent-repo for hash-anchored file edits and LSP-backed context.
three_beat_structure: Hook: A coding model that fits in 4GB of RAM and still ships working diffs.
Payoff: Live test on a Pi 4 — clone, load, and ask it to fix a real bug.
CTA: Pull the model, wire it to your terminal agent, and see if it outperforms your current setup.
demo_segment: SSH into a Pi 4, pull the model from HuggingFace, pipe a broken Python function through the agent harness, and show the diff output with wall-clock timing.
caveats: Description for acme/coder-model is empty on HuggingFace. Benchmark numbers and exact parameter count are unconfirmed. Agent integration assumes acme/agent-repo tool harness.
closing_takeaway: The ceiling on small coding models is higher than most people think. Test it before you write it off.
call_to_action: Clone acme/agent-repo, plug in coder-model, post your tok/s and diff quality.
short_script: acme/coder-model dropped on HuggingFace and it is tiny enough for a Pi 4. Clustered with an agent harness and a paper on practical workflows. The catch is there is no detailed spec yet so you have to test it blind. Still, a model this small that ships real diffs is worth a weekend project.
visual_idea: Split screen: HuggingFace model card on the left, Pi 4 terminal running an agent loop on the right.
cta: Pull the model and post your results.

suggested_titles.curiosity: The coding model that fits in 4GB and still works
suggested_titles.practical: acme/coder-model: a local coding agent you can run tonight
suggested_titles.contrarian: You don't need a 70B parameter model to ship code
suggested_titles.tutorial: How to run acme/coder-model on a Pi 4 with acme/agent-repo

broll_list: HuggingFace model card page for acme/coder-model with download count
Pi 4 board with terminal showing model loading in under 30 seconds
Side-by-side diff of broken code vs. model-suggested fix
insight: Model size is not capability. The smallest model you can actually run locally is worth more than the best model behind an API. Distribution matters more than ceiling.
hooks: A coding model that runs on a Pi 4 should not work. It does.
The agent workflow paper that changes how you think about small models.
Ship local code agents with a model that fits in 4GB.
